chapter_title.ar: بَابُ ثَبَاتِ الْإِمَامَةِ فِي الْأَعْقَابِ وَ أَنَّهَا لَا تَعُودُ فِي أَخٍ وَ لَا عَمٍّ وَ لَا غَيْرِهِمَا مِنَ الْقَرَابَاتِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 63 – The continuation of the Imamate be in the descendants and that it would not be returning to be in a brother, nor a paternal uncle nor any other from the relatives

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/212)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/212)

parts.text: عِيسَى بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ ( عليه السلام )

text: قُلْتُ لَهُ إِنْ كَانَ كَوْنٌ وَ لَا أَرَانِي اللَّهُ فَبِمَنْ أَئْتَمُّ فَأَوْمَأَ إِلَى ابْنِهِ مُوسَى

قَالَ قُلْتُ فَإِنْ حَدَثَ بِمُوسَى حَدَثٌ فَبِمَنْ أَئْتَمُّ قَالَ بِوَلَدِهِ قُلْتُ فَإِنْ حَدَثَ بِوَلَدِهِ حَدَثٌ وَ تَرَكَ أَخاً كَبِيراً وَ ابْناً صَغِيراً فَبِمَنْ أَئْتَمُّ قَالَ بِوَلَدِهِ ثُمَّ وَاحِداً فَوَاحِداً .
وَ فِي نُسْخَةِ الصَّفْوَانِيِّ ثُمَّ هَكَذَا أَبَداً .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Ibn Abu Najran,

(It has been narrated) from Isa Bin Abdullah Bin Umar Bin Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Abu Talib<sup>asws</sup>, from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘If what is to happen does happen, may Allah<sup>azwj</sup> not Show me (that), so with whom should I take as an Imam<sup>asws</sup>?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> gestured towards his<sup>asws</sup> son<sup>asws</sup> Musa<sup>asws</sup>’.

He (the narrator) said, ‘I said, ‘So if is an event (of death) occurs with Musa<sup>asws</sup>, so with whom shall I take as an Imam<sup>asws</sup>?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘With his<sup>asws</sup> son<sup>asws</sup>’. I said, ‘So if an event (of death) occurs with his<sup>asws</sup> son<sup>asws</sup>, and he<sup>asws</sup> leaves a lot of brothers, or young sons, so with whom shall I take as an Imam<sup>asws</sup>?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘By his<sup>asws</sup> son<sup>asws</sup>, then one after one’.
And in a copy of Al-Safwany, (He<sup>asws</sup> said): ‘Then it would be like this, forever!’
en.sarwar: 5. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from ibn abu Najran from ‘Isa ibn ‘Abdallah ibn ‘Umar ibn Ali ibn abu Talib (a.s.) who has said that he asked abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) the following. “Would there happen what is to happen, may Allah would not show me such a day (death of the Imam (a.s.) who then must I follow as my Imam?” The Imam (a.s.) pointed to his son Musa (a.s.). The narrator has said that he asked the Imams (a.s.), “ What if something will happen to Musa (a.s.) who then must I follow?” The Imam (a.s.), “Follow a son.” I then asked, “What if something would happen to the son and the Imam would leave behind an elder brother or a small son then who must I follow?” The Imam (a.s.) said, “Follow his son and so on one after the other.” In the script of Sawan it says, “. .and so on forever.”
